prompts: Bacteria are classified into various categories based on characteristics such as cell wall composition, shape, metabolism, and genetic makeup. The primary classifications are based on Gram staining, dividing bacteria into Gram-positive and Gram-negative groups. Structural shapes include cocci (spherical), bacilli (rod-shaped), and spirilla (spiral). Bacteria can also be categorized as aerobic or anaerobic based on their oxygen requirements, and autotrophic or heterotrophic based on their nutritional needs. The classification system can include higher levels like domain and kingdom under prokaryotes, with further divisions into phyla, classes, orders, families, genera, and species.
